Hi Guys,

I trigger the gating on this one once again as it failed but before we proceed with this change could you please:

1) Clearly describe why this version bump is not applicable to master

or

2) Submit the patch to master first and we will merge it after that one gets merged

Patch Set 7: Code-Review+1

> Patch Set 7: Code-Review-2
> 
> Hi Guys,
> 
> I trigger the gating on this one once again as it failed but before we proceed with this change could you please:
> 
> 1) Clearly describe why this version bump is not applicable to master
> 
> or
> 
> 2) Submit the patch to master first and we will merge it after that one gets merged

We take the approach that changes that are only destined for Honolulu (or Honolulu Maintenance) should only be made on the honolulu branch.  There is no benefit in pushing it to master first.  That's the approach we've taken with the Policy repos and see no reason to treat OOM any differently.  With OOM, in particular, pushing to master first would be a serious impediment, given how long it takes to get reviews merged.

That being said, we will likely push a review for the master branch next week, but that will contain a first cut at images for Istanbul, all of which will have different version numbers than they do in honolulu.  (The property files used within the pods will likely be different, too.)

Patch Set 7:

looks like the last failure was in xacml-pdp, as usual

Patch Set 7: Code-Review+1

I completely agree with Jim. 
And I believe "master" branch is pointing to Istanbul release (as usual). Considering that it is maintenance release of Honolulu, it has to be done via updating "honolulu" branch.

Patch Set 7: Code-Review+1

Healthchecks pass in last result set above.   OOM and policy master branches have differed considerably at this point compared with honolulu as both projects are in midst of istanbul development, not sure how comparable the results would be, and what the additional work and subsequent delays that that would require to put in master and then back in honolulu would buy.   We have run regression tests in honolulu in windriver and Bell C. labs with OOM official honolulu installation if that makes folks more comfortable.    This is a better release for policy in Honolulu as more importantly it solves some unadvertised changes in a 3rd party CDS library that we only found out recently.  We also have a company lined up to pick up this maintenance release with policy changes when released that requires the CDS issue resolution.   Hopefully this additional background info would help making our ONAP teammates more confident in accepting this submission.
